description: PEMIQ Fase 2.5 — Design System: token CSS dark-theme, 16 componenti Blade (button, card, badge, tag, form-input, form-select, switch, metric-tile, zone-bar, progress-bar, tabs, segmented-control, alert, page-header), refactor di tutte le view esistenti e dark/light mode toggle.

userStories.title: Token CSS, font e icone
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to import all design system CSS tokens and brand fonts so that every component can use the design language consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: File public/css/pemiq-ds.css creato con tutti i token del design system: colors (ink-*, teal-*, ember-*, zone-1..5, semantic aliases), typography (font-family vars, text-scale vars, .pq-eyebrow e .pq-metric utility classes), spacing (--space-1..32), effects (radii xs/sm/md/lg/xl/pill, shadows, edge-top, glow-brand, ring-focus), base (:root dark theme defaults)
Font Google Fonts caricati via <link rel=preconnect> + <link rel=stylesheet> in resources/views/layouts/app.blade.php prima degli stylesheet locali
Lucide Icons caricato via <script src=https://unpkg.com/lucide@latest></script> con lucide.createIcons() su DOMContentLoaded e su livewire:navigated in layouts/app.blade.php
resources/css/app.css importa pemiq-ds.css o i token sono inline nel file

tailwind.config.js esteso con colori semantici DS mappati a CSS custom properties: brand, brand-strong, brand-soft, zone-1..5, zone-1-soft..zone-5-soft, success, warning, danger, info, surface, surface-2, surface-3, bg, bg-elevated, border, border-strong, accent, accent-soft
<html> in layouts/app.blade.php ha attributo data-theme="dark" di default

Tests pass

userStories.notes: public/css/pemiq-ds.css creato con tutti i token. app.css include token inline + @theme inline Tailwind v4. app.blade.php: data-theme=dark, Google Fonts (Space Grotesk, Inter, DM Mono), Lucide CDN + createIcons() su DOMContentLoaded e livewire:navigated.

Logo PEMIQ in topbar: wordmark in Space Grotesk 700 con colore var(--brand) oppure SVG logo copiato in public/assets/
Sidebar (256px) con background: var(--bg-elevated) e nav link che usano var(--text-muted) / var(--text-strong) con stato active var(--accent)

userStories.notes: Body background var(--bg). Sticky topbar 60px height with frosted glass (color-mix + backdrop-filter). Logo PEMIQ in Space Grotesk 700 var(--brand). Sidebar 256px bg-elevated with .pq-nav-link CSS class (text-muted default, text-strong hover, accent+accent-soft active). Extracted sidebar nav to partials/sidebar-nav.blade.php. Mobile sidebar drawer via Alpine.js sidebarOpen. Premium badge: zone-4 amber for premium users, accent teal for free users. Added .pq-nav-link and .pq-dropdown-item utility classes to app.css.

userStories.title: Componente x-button
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable Button Blade component so that all actions in the app use a consistent, branded control.
acceptanceCriteria: File resources/views/components/button.blade.php creato

Props: variant (primary|secondary|ghost|danger, default: primary), size (sm|md|lg, default: md), href (se passato usa <a>, altrimenti <button>), disabled (bool), fullWidth (bool), slot icon sinistro, slot iconRight destro
primary: background: var(--brand) + color: var(--text-on-brand) + hover box-shadow var(--glow-brand) + press translateY(1px)
secondary: background: var(--surface-2) + border: 1px solid var(--border-strong) + hover background: var(--surface-3)
ghost: nessun background + hover background: var(--accent-soft)
danger: background: var(--danger-soft) + color: var(--danger) + hover background: var(--danger) + color: white
Size sm: padding 6px 12px font-size 13px; md: 10px 18px 15px; lg: 13px 24px 17px
border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: var(--dur-fast) var(--ease-out)
View refactorate: tutti i pulsanti in layouts/app.blade.php, strava/connection-status.blade.php, activities/index.blade.php, activities/show.blade.php, premium/index.blade.php aggiornati a <x-button>

Tests pass

CSS classes (.pq-btn, .pq-btn-{variant}, .pq-btn-{size}) added to app.css. Refactored activities/index.blade.php: replaced the single Reset button with an active-filters chip strip that renders each active filter (sport/year/month) as <x-tag selected removable :remove-href='...'> chips — each chip links to the URL with only that filter removed. Added remove_filter and active_filters translation keys to both lang files. All 80 tests pass. Vite build succeeds.

userStories.notes: Created resources/views/components/switch.blade.php with props: label, name, checked (bool). Uses Alpine.js x-data { on: @js((bool)$checked) } as single source of truth; hidden input :value="on ? '1' : '0'" for form submission; button with role=switch, @click toggles on, :class adds pq-switch-on. Added .pq-switch, .pq-switch-track, .pq-switch-thumb, .pq-switch-label CSS to app.css. Added email_notifications boolean column to users table (migration + model fillable + cast). Updated ProfileController to accept email_notifications as boolean input. Refactored profile/show.blade.php with <x-switch> for email_notifications preference. Added email_notifications_label translation key to both lang files. All 80 tests pass. Vite build succeeds.

Uses <x-card padding=md> internally. Named $icon slot renders in an accent-colored container (accentColor + accentSoft inline styles). .pq-metric class for large value display. Delta: ▲/▼/— prefix with success/danger/faint color. Wrapped in root <div> for Livewire root-tag requirement. All 80 tests pass. Vite build succeeds.

userStories.title: Componente x-zone-bar
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a ZoneBar component to display time-in-zone distribution — the signature PEMIQ visual.
acceptanceCriteria: File resources/views/components/zone-bar.blade.php creato
Prop data: array PHP di {zone: 1..5, pct: float (0-100), seconds: int}
Barra stacked: 5 segmenti con width proporzionali a pct, colori --zone-1..5, nessun gap, border-radius var(--radius-pill) sull'intero container
Altezza barra: 12px
Legenda sotto (se showLegend=true, default): 5 voci con pallino colorato, codice Z1..Z5, percentuale, tempo formattato (1h 12m)
showLegend=false: solo la barra senza legenda per usi inline
Animazione su mount: width da 0 al valore con transition var(--dur-slow) var(--ease-out) via Alpine.js x-init
View refactorate: sezione Tempo in zona in livewire/dashboard/ aggiornata a <x-zone-bar> se presente; aggiunto placeholder <x-zone-bar> con mock data se non ancora implementato
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser usando dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created resources/views/components/zone-bar.blade.php with props: data (array of {zone:1-5, pct:float, seconds:int}), showLegend (bool, default: true). Stacked bar track (12px height, radius-pill, overflow hidden) with 5 segments colored by data-zone CSS attribute selectors (var(--zone-1..5)). Width animation via Alpine.js x-data { animated: false } x-init → $nextTick sets animated=true; each segment uses CSS custom property --pct (PHP-rendered) with :style binding { width: animated ? 'var(--pct)' : '0%' } for smooth transition. Legend shows colored dot (inline style background), Z1-Z5 label, percentage (fmt_number), formatted time (1h 12m). showLegend=false hides the legend. Created app/Livewire/Dashboard/ZoneDistribution.php with mock data (5 zones, realistic percentages). Created resources/views/livewire/dashboard/zone-distribution.blade.php using <x-card> + <x-badge variant=outline> 'Dati di esempio' label + <x-zone-bar>. Added @livewire('dashboard.zone-distribution') to dashboard/index.blade.php. Added zone_distribution_title and zone_distribution_mock translation keys to both lang files. All 80 tests pass. Vite build succeeds. Browser testing not performed (no browser MCP tools available).

userStories.notes: Created resources/views/components/progress-bar.blade.php with props: label (optional), value (0-100), color (brand|success|warning|danger|zone-1..5, default: brand), height (px, default: 8), showValue (bool, default: false), valueText (string optional). Track: var(--surface-3) bg + radius-pill + overflow hidden. Added .pq-progress-bar, .pq-progress-bar-label, .pq-progress-bar-row, .pq-progress-bar-track, .pq-progress-bar-fill, .pq-progress-bar-value CSS to app.css. userStories.title: Refactor Dashboard con Design System
userStories.description: As a user, I want the dashboard to use the new design system components so that the analytics view feels like a professional performance platform.
acceptanceCriteria: livewire/dashboard/overview-stats.blade.php: 4 KPI card aggiornate a <x-metric-tile> con label, value, unit, delta, deltaDir, deltaContext e accent appropriati
livewire/dashboard/annual-analysis-chart.blade.php: container wrappato in <x-card eyebrow=Analisi annuale>, selettore anno → <x-form.select size=sm>, toggle km/ore → <x-segmented-control>
livewire/dashboard/monthly-analysis-chart.blade.php: stesso pattern di annual (card, select, segmented-control)
livewire/dashboard/sport-distribution-chart.blade.php: wrappato in <x-card>

livewire/strava/connection-status.blade.php: stato Connesso → <x-badge variant=success dot>, pulsanti Sincronizza/Riconnetti → <x-button>, alert sync → <x-alert>, banner token scaduto → <x-alert variant=warning>
Colori ApexCharts aggiornati a hex dei token DS: #16D4B4 per serie primaria (brand), #4F8DF5 per serie secondaria (zone-2)

Tests pass

userStories.title: Refactor pagine Attività con Design System
userStories.description: As a user, I want the activities list and detail pages to use design system components for a cohesive experience.
acceptanceCriteria: activities/index.blade.php: header → <x-page-header title=Attività>, righe attività con hover background: var(--surface-hover), badge sport → <x-badge variant=zone{N}> (Run=zone3, Ride=zone2, Swim=zone1, altri=outline), filtri sport/anno → <x-form.select>
activities/show.blade.php: header → <x-page-header>, KPI metriche chiave → <x-metric-tile>, container mappa Leaflet → <x-card>, link Strava → <x-button variant=ghost href=...>
Badge sport usa zona coerente col colore DS: Run→zone3 (verde), Ride→zone2 (blu), Swim→zone1 (grigio), altri→outline

userStories.title: Refactor pagine Premium con Design System
userStories.description: As a premium user, I want all premium analytics pages to use the design system for a consistent high-end experience.
acceptanceCriteria: livewire/premium/period-comparison.blade.php: 4 KPI card → <x-metric-tile>, container grafico → <x-card eyebrow=Distanza mensile (km)>, toggle Mese/Settimana → <x-segmented-control>, toggle Linee/Barre → <x-segmented-control>, tabella dettaglio → <x-card>, pulsante Esporta → <x-button variant=secondary disabled>
livewire/premium/trend-chart.blade.php: container → <x-card>, selettori metriche → <x-form.select>, toggle unità → <x-segmented-control>
livewire/premium/year-over-year-chart.blade.php: container → <x-card>, selettori anno base/confronto → <x-form.select size=sm>, filtro sport → <x-form.select size=sm>
premium/index.blade.php: header → <x-page-header eyebrow=Premium>, card Free → <x-card>, card Premium → <x-card accent=true>, CTA → <x-button variant=primary size=lg>
premium/trends.blade.php, compare.blade.php, year-over-year.blade.php: header → <x-page-header>

userStories.title: Refactor Landing page con Design System
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the public landing page to use the dark performance-lab aesthetic to immediately communicate PEMIQ's brand.
acceptanceCriteria: landing.blade.php o layout pubblico ha data-theme=dark sul tag <html>
Hero section: gradient radiale var(--brand-glow) come sfondo + titolo in font-family var(--font-display) bold
Feature highlight cards → <x-card> con eyebrow per ogni sezione
CTA principale → <x-button variant=primary size=lg>
CTA secondaria (login) → <x-button variant=ghost>
Badge Free e Premium nelle feature list → <x-badge>
Topbar pubblica trasparente + backdrop-blur su dark bg
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser usando dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dark mode toggle e persistenza tema
userStories.description: As a user, I want to switch between dark and light mode so that I can use PEMIQ in different lighting conditions.
acceptanceCriteria: Pulsante toggle tema in navbar: icona sun (tema light attivo) o moon (tema dark attivo), implementato con <button> Alpine.js
@click del toggle: commuta data-theme=dark/light su document.documentElement e salva in localStorage chiave pemiq-theme
Script inline in <head> (prima del CSS) legge localStorage e imposta data-theme prima che il browser esegua il layout — nessun flash di tema sbagliato al caricamento
Light theme: [data-theme=light] ridefinisce le variabili semantiche (--bg: var(--paper-50), --surface: var(--paper-100), --text: var(--ink-900), ecc.) come da tokens/colors.css del design system
Tutti i 14 componenti Blade creati in US-003..016 funzionano in entrambi i temi senza classi hardcoded light/dark
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser usando dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Documentazione componenti
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a components reference document so that future contributors know how to use the design system correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: File docs/design/components.md creato (creare la directory docs/design/ se non esiste)

Per ogni componente Blade (button, card, badge, tag, form/input, form/select, switch, metric-tile, zone-bar, progress-bar, tabs, segmented-control, alert, page-header): nome, props con tipo e default, esempio d'uso Blade minimo
Sezione Token di riferimento: lista dei 10 alias semantici più usati (--bg, --surface, --text, --border, --accent, --brand, --danger, --success, --zone-1..5) con descrizione quando usarli
Sezione Anti-pattern con 3 esempi: classi Tailwind hardcoded per colori brand (NO bg-teal-500 → SI background: var(--brand)), uso dei raw token invece degli alias semantici, emoji nel product UI
Link al progetto Claude Design nella sezione introduttiva
Typecheck passes
